title: Censored AFT model for laps-until-next-pit
summary: The target is a one-step slice of an underlying time-to-event problem.
rationale: The target is a one-step slice of an underlying time-to-event problem. XGBoost’s AFT objective is designed for censored event times, which matches rows near the end of a race or final stints where the next pit may never occur in-observation. Converting predicted remaining-lap time into next-lap hazard should add a lower-correlation expert relative to pure binary classifiers.
implementation_hint: Rebuild labels per row as remaining laps until the driver’s next pit in the same race; rows with no future pit become right-censored. Train an `xgb.train` model with `objective=survival:aft`, `label_lower_bound`, and `label_upper_bound`. At inference, transform predicted survival time into `P(T<=1)` or a monotone proxy for next-lap probability, then rank-blend with the current XGB/CatBoost classifier stack.
expected_effect: Could improve ranking on long-tail stints and end-of-race cases where binary models overfit local heuristics instead of the full stop-time structure.
risk: Main trap is label construction leakage: future pits must be computed only within each row’s own race trajectory, while all feature engineering remains strictly past/current. Raw AFT outputs may need fold-safe monotone calibration before blending.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
